AlmostThere-Numeric.otf: Numeric font based on the x-wing targeting computer
AquabeshAF-Regular.otf: Casual Aurebesh font featuring unique upper and lowercase letters.
ataribesh.ttf: An 8-bit Aurebesh font based on classic videogame fonts
ataribesh-sprites.ttf: An 8-bit Aurebesh font based on classic videogame fonts
ataribesh-subtitles.ttf: An 8-bit Aurebesh font based on classic videogame fonts
ataribesh-titles.ttf: An 8-bit Aurebesh font based on classic videogame fonts
AttackPosition-Symbols.otf: Symbolic font based on console buttons from Star Wars, Rogue One, and Solo
AURABESH.ttf: The first Aurebesh font; small lowercase; basic nums; non-canon punctuation & symbols
Auraboo.otf: "Naboo Aurebesh" font based on Naboo Starfighter screen in The Phantom Menace; basic nums
AurebeshAF-Canon.otf: Clean, robust Aurebesh font with options for digraphs and tech numerals
AurebeshBloopsAF.otf: A bubbly display variant of Aurebesh
Clynese_Bend.otf: Clynese variant for streetsigns; letter y is upside down; non-canon nums, based on Sabacc dice and cards
Clynese_Hand.otf: Clynese variant for streetsigns; letter y is upside down; non-canon nums, based on Sabacc dice and cards
Cyrillibesh-BETA.otf: Basic Aurebesh english, with additional non-canonical letters for writing Russian Aurebesh
DockingBay94-Display.otf: Aurebesh font based on the docking bay signage at Mos Eisley Spaceport (Star Wars the Motion Picture, and The Mandalorian S1)
DomabeshConsole.otf: Basic Domabesh font; digraphs mapped to symbol keys; tech nums
ewok.TTF: Legends Ewokese font based on symbols in the cartoon series
FresianAlphabetAF.otf: Based on stenciled text on the exterior of the X-wings in the original trilogy
GeonosianPM.otf: Geonosian font based on Philip Metschan's original design
ImperialBroadcast87.otf: Based on a 1987 broadcast of The Empire Strikes Back
IthorianAF.otf: Based on sign text seen on Coruscant in Attack of the Clones and on Takobo in Rebels
KyberCrystalDisplay-Aurebesh.otf: For security consoles; includes Aurebesh version
KyberCrystalDisplay-Alphabet.otf: For security consoles; includes Alphabet version
LaptiNekAF.otf: Aurebesh typeface inspired by geometric fonts like Disco and Sinaloa
LifeDayAFBETA.otf: Font from the opening titles of The Faithful Wookiee animated segment of the Star Wars Holiday Special (1978)
MandoAF-Classic.otf: Mandalorian font based Philip Metschan's Attack of the Clones design
MandoAF-Regular.otf: Mandalorian font based on the Visual Dicitonary. Non-canon punctuation.
MB450.otf: Numeric font based Luke's MB 450 Macrobinoculars (Numbers and Symbols)
NabooAFAurebesh-Regular.otf: A "Naboo Aurebesh" font based on lettering seen throughout the Prequel Trilogy
naboo.TTF: Futhark font based on the markings on Naboo Starfighters in the Phantom Menace
nal-huttese.ttf: Outer Rim Basic font misidentified as Huttese, based on glyphs seen in The Phantom Menace
nal-huttese-99.ttf: Outer Rim Basic font misidentified as Huttese, based on glyphs seen in The Phantom Menace
newAurabesh.ttf: Standard Aurebesh font; small tech nums
OldTongue_Yavin.otf: Basic Old Tongue font; non-canon punctuation; non-canon tech nums; letter U is a little off
OuterRimAF-Regular.otf: Outer Rim Basic font, featuring full canonical alphabet and numerals
PrimeJedi.otf: Old Tongue font based on merchandise sold at Galaxy's Edge
ProtobeshAF.otf: Protobesh font based on inscriptions in the Sacred Jedi Texts; non-canon nums
RememberKamino.otf: Based on the logo for "Republic Commando"
SFoil-Regular.otf: Unknown script seen on Luke's X-wing console in Ep V and VI as well as various Rebel and Resistance screens in Ep VIII, Ep IX, and Rogue One
SithAF-BETA.otf: Sith Kittât font; contains all 311 character combinations as ligatures; backwards compatible with most romanized Kittât
SkyLuke.otf: Based upon Luke Skywalker's signature in the book "The Jedi Path" by Daniel Wallace
TFGunray-Bold.otf: Trade Federation font, based on the Visual Encyclopedia; complete alphabet and nums
ubese-boushh-beta.ttf: "Ubese" font based on Boushh's clothing; limited glyphs; some non-canon glyphs; no nums
ANewHopeRegular.ttf: 1998 font based on the Star Wars logo
RogueAF.otf: Recreation of button icons, primarily from Star Wars, Rogue One, and Solo
sthproph.ttf: Font based on the unidentified writing on every known version of Darth Vader's chestbox
